sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 68-year-old male, presents with a 2-hour history of lower back pain and tingling sensation in the right leg.
He was raking leaves in his yard when he first experienced pain, which worsened upon standing and bending.
He has had similar back pain in the past, but it resolved on its own within a day.
He has not noticed any prior pain in the affected area.
His partner reports similar pain upon standing, which is a new symptom.
The patient's pain is exacerbated by bending and palpation of the l5 area.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient's dorsiflexion is normal, and pulses are equal in all extremities.
The patient experiences pain upon palpation of the l5 area and when standing.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient likely has a sprained low back.
Problem list: Acute low back strain; suspected lumbar sprain; [NOT MENTIONED]
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 7.5mg orally twice a day for pain management.
I also prescribed physical therapy to help with stretches and exercises to improve mobility and reduce pain.
The patient is to rest the affected area and follow up with me in two weeks if the pain does not improve.
If the pain persists, we will consider an MRI to rule out any underlying conditions.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me in two weeks to re-evaluate the pain and determine the next course of action.
